content.text: Launch Readiness Tasks and Owner Assignments

A launch readiness checklist without owner assignments is a list of wishes. Every task must have a named owner who is accountable for completing it, a deadline expressed as days before launch (T-minus), and a clear definition of done that is verifiable. For GenAI services, the task set spans five stakeholder groups and a 14-day preparation window.

The T-minus framing matters because tasks have dependencies: the eval gate (T-14) must finish before the security review can assess output safety (T-7), which must be complete before waivers can be approved (T-3), which must be resolved before the engineering lead grants the final gate (T-1). Collapsing the window by starting too late causes all tasks to pile up at T-1 and creates launch-day pressure that leads to skipped steps.

attrs.alt: Launch readiness timeline with owner lanes from T-14 to launch

content.text: The Master Launch Checklist

Each item specifies the owner role, the T-minus deadline, the definition of done, and the evidence artifact that proves completion. Assign every item to a named individual at the start of the launch preparation window.

## Launch Readiness Checklist — GenAI Service

Service: ___  Version: ___  Launch date: ___  Coordinator: ___

### T-14 Days

- [ ] [SRE] Load test at 2x peak. Done: P99 latency <= SLO in report. Evidence: CI job URL.
- [ ] [ML Eng] Full eval suite run on prod model+prompt. Done: >= 95% pass or waiver drafted.
- [ ] [ML Eng] Eval suite covers all intent categories. Done: Coverage matrix signed by PM.
- [ ] [Eng Lead] PRR tracker opened with all five sign-off rows. Done: Launch ticket created.
- [ ] [Security] Threat model documented. Done: Covers injection, exfiltration, output abuse.

### T-7 Days

- [ ] [SRE] Runbook dry-run with >=2 engineers. Done: Notes attached to runbook doc.
- [ ] [ML Eng] Shadow eval deployed to staging. Done: quality_score gauge active in Grafana.
- [ ] [Security] Security review complete. Done: prr_security_check.py passes clean.
- [ ] [FinOps] Cost projection (P50+P99). Done: Approved daily budget set in cost-alerts.yaml.
- [ ] [Legal/PM] Output content policy reviewed. Done: PM sign-off comment in ticket.

### T-3 Days

- [ ] [ML Eng] Model version pinned (no "latest"). Done: Explicit version in model-config.prod.yaml.
- [ ] [SRE] On-call rotation set (>=2 engineers). Done: PagerDuty escalation policy active.
- [ ] [Security] Output validator tested with adversarial inputs. Done: >=10 injection cases blocked.
- [ ] [FinOps] Budget alerts configured. Done: Daily cap + hourly spike alert tested.
- [ ] [Eng Lead] All open waivers approved. Done: prr-waivers.json all APPROVED.

### T-1 Day

- [ ] [SRE] Production dashboard live. Done: All four panels showing data (latency, tokens, quality, fallback).
- [ ] [ML Eng] Eval sign-off in PRR tracker. Done: Comment with eval run URL + pass rate.
- [ ] [FinOps] FinOps sign-off in PRR tracker. Done: Approver comment in launch ticket.
- [ ] [Eng Lead] Final gate: all_hard_blockers_green: true in prr-status.json.
- [ ] [Eng Lead] Launch comms sent to stakeholders and support team.

### Launch Day (T-0)

- [ ] [SRE] Traffic shift initiated. Done: First prod requests visible in dashboard.
- [ ] [SRE+ML] First 30min: monitor live. Done: No alerts in first 30 minutes.
- [ ] [Eng Lead] Launch confirmed. Done: Metrics stable at T+30min, post-launch monitoring active.
Automating Checklist Status from CI

Rather than manually ticking boxes, wire CI jobs to write their results into a machine-readable status file. The launch ticket then links to a generated status page rather than relying on humans to remember to update it.

# scripts/update_launch_status.py
# Called by each CI domain job to record its result.
# Maintains launch-status.json which the final gate reads.

import json, sys, datetime, pathlib

STATUS_FILE = pathlib.Path("launch-status.json")

TASK_SCHEMA = {
    "load_test":          {"owner_role": "SRE",      "deadline": "T-14", "hard_blocker": True},
    "eval_gate":          {"owner_role": "ML Eng",   "deadline": "T-14", "hard_blocker": True},
    "threat_model":       {"owner_role": "Security", "deadline": "T-14", "hard_blocker": False},
    "runbook_dry_run":    {"owner_role": "SRE",      "deadline": "T-7",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "shadow_eval_deploy": {"owner_role": "ML Eng",   "deadline": "T-7",  "hard_blocker": False},
    "security_review":    {"owner_role": "Security", "deadline": "T-7",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "cost_projection":    {"owner_role": "FinOps",   "deadline": "T-7",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "model_version_pin":  {"owner_role": "ML Eng",   "deadline": "T-3",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "oncall_rotation":    {"owner_role": "SRE",      "deadline": "T-3",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "budget_alerts":      {"owner_role": "FinOps",   "deadline": "T-3",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "output_validator":   {"owner_role": "Security", "deadline": "T-3",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "prr_waivers":        {"owner_role": "Eng Lead", "deadline": "T-3",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "monitoring_dash":    {"owner_role": "SRE",      "deadline": "T-1",  "hard_blocker": True},
    "final_prr_gate":     {"owner_role": "Eng Lead", "deadline": "T-1",  "hard_blocker": True},
}

def load_status() -> dict:
    if STATUS_FILE.exists():
        return json.loads(STATUS_FILE.read_text())
    return {"tasks": {}, "all_hard_blockers_green": False}

def record_task(task_id: str, status: str, evidence_url: str, run_by: str) -> None:
    if task_id not in TASK_SCHEMA:
        print(f"Unknown task_id: {task_id}. Valid: {list(TASK_SCHEMA)}")
        sys.exit(1)
    state = load_status()
    state["tasks"][task_id] = {
        "status": status,
        "evidence_url": evidence_url,
        "run_by": run_by,
        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
        **TASK_SCHEMA[task_id],
    }
    hard_blockers = [k for k, v in TASK_SCHEMA.items() if v["hard_blocker"]]
    state["all_hard_blockers_green"] = all(
        state["tasks"].get(t, {}).get("status") == "PASS"
        for t in hard_blockers
    )
    state["last_updated"] = datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z"
    STATUS_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2))
    print(f"[{task_id}] {status} by {run_by} — hard_blockers_green={state['all_hard_blockers_green']}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    record_task(*sys.argv[1:5])

# .github/workflows/launch-final-gate.yml
# Blocks production deploy unless all hard-blocker tasks are PASS in launch-status.json.
name: Launch Final Gate

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      confirm:
        description: "Type LAUNCH to confirm production deploy"
        required: true

jobs:
  final-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production   # requires manual approval in GitHub Environments
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Verify launch status
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.confirm }}" != "LAUNCH" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: Type LAUNCH to confirm. Got: ${{ github.event.inputs.confirm }}"
            exit 1
          fi
          python3 -c "
          import json, sys
          s = json.load(open('launch-status.json'))
          if not s.get('all_hard_blockers_green'):
              pending = [k for k,v in s['tasks'].items() if v['hard_blocker'] and v['status'] != 'PASS']
              print('LAUNCH BLOCKED. Pending hard-blocker tasks:', pending)
              sys.exit(1)
          print('All hard-blocker tasks PASS. Final gate: OPEN.')
          "

      - name: Record final gate
        run: python3 scripts/update_launch_status.py final_prr_gate PASS "$GITHUB_SERVER_URL/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/actions/runs/$GITHUB_RUN_ID" "@$GITHUB_ACTOR"
Handling Late or Unassigned Tasks

The most common failure mode in launch readiness is a task that has no named owner. 'SRE' is not a person — '@alice' is. If any checklist item has no @handle assigned after T-12, the PRR coordinator escalates to the stakeholder group's manager immediately.

# scripts/check_launch_assignments.py
# Run at T-12 to catch unassigned tasks before they become blockers.

import json, sys, pathlib

ASSIGNMENTS_FILE = pathlib.Path("launch-assignments.json")
# Format: {"load_test": "@alice", "eval_gate": "@bob", ...}

if not ASSIGNMENTS_FILE.exists():
    print("ERROR: launch-assignments.json not found.")
    print("Create it mapping each task_id to a @handle.")
    sys.exit(1)

assignments = json.loads(ASSIGNMENTS_FILE.read_text())

REQUIRED_TASKS = [
    "load_test", "eval_gate", "threat_model", "runbook_dry_run",
    "shadow_eval_deploy", "security_review", "cost_projection",
    "model_version_pin", "oncall_rotation", "budget_alerts",
    "output_validator", "prr_waivers", "monitoring_dash", "final_prr_gate",
]

errors = []
for task_id in REQUIRED_TASKS:
    owner = assignments.get(task_id, "")
    if not owner:
        errors.append(f"UNASSIGNED: {task_id}")
    elif not owner.startswith("@"):
        errors.append(f"INVALID: {task_id} = {owner!r} (must be a @handle)")

if errors:
    print("LAUNCH ASSIGNMENT GAPS:")
    for e in errors:
        print(f"  {e}")
    sys.exit(1)

print(f"All {len(REQUIRED_TASKS)} tasks have named owners. PASS.")

Run check_launch_assignments.py as the first step of the T-14 kickoff meeting. If any task has no owner by end of that meeting, the PRR coordinator assigns one before the meeting ends — never leave the room with an unowned task.
Launch-Day Communication Protocol

The launch-day itself requires a structured communication protocol so all stakeholders know what is happening in real time. A launch without a comms plan results in support teams learning about the launch from customer tickets, and executives learning about problems only after they become public.

## Launch-Day Comms Template

### T-24h: Pre-launch announcement (Eng Lead sends)
To: engineering-all@, support-team@, product-team@
Subject: [LAUNCH TOMORROW] genai-chat-service v1.0

We are launching genai-chat-service v1.0 tomorrow at 10:00 UTC.

**What's changing:** LLM-powered chat assistant on the /chat endpoint.
**Traffic rollout:** 5% canary for 30 minutes, then 100% if metrics are green.
**Rollback plan:** Feature flag llm_killswitch=true immediately stops LLM calls.
**Monitoring:** [Dashboard URL] — @sre-alice is watching live.
**If something goes wrong:** Page @sre-alice via PagerDuty.

### T-0: Launch started (PRR coordinator posts in #incidents-live)
"[LAUNCH] genai-chat-service v1.0 — traffic shift starting now. Monitoring: [URL]"

### T+30min: Launch confirmed (PRR coordinator posts in #incidents-live)
"[LAUNCH CONFIRMED] genai-chat-service v1.0 — metrics stable. No alerts.
 P99: Xs | Quality: X% | Token cost: $X/hr | Fallback rate: X%"

### T+24h: Launch summary (Eng Lead sends)
To: engineering-all@, support-team@, product-team@, exec-team@
Subject: [LAUNCH COMPLETE] genai-chat-service v1.0 — first 24h metrics

First 24h metrics:
- Total requests: X
- P99 latency: Xs (SLO target: Xs) — [GREEN/AMBER/RED]
- Quality score: X% (floor: 90%) — [GREEN/AMBER/RED]
- LLM API cost: $X (daily budget: $X) — [GREEN/AMBER/RED]
- User-reported issues: X

Next review: 7-day post-launch check scheduled for [date] with @sre-alice + @ml-bob.
Go/No-Go Decision at T-0

Even with a perfect PRR, the decision to send traffic is made by a human at T-0. The go/no-go call uses a simple three-question checklist: Are all dashboards showing baseline data? Has the canary deployment completed successfully? Are all on-call engineers reachable? A NO on any question means a no-go until resolved.

# scripts/go_no_go.py
# Interactive go/no-go checklist run by the PRR coordinator at T-0.
# Writes the decision to launch-status.json.

import json, sys, datetime, pathlib

def ask(question: str) -> bool:
    while True:
        ans = input(f"{question} [yes/no]: ").strip().lower()
        if ans in ("yes", "y"):
            return True
        if ans in ("no", "n"):
            return False

print("=== GO / NO-GO CHECKLIST ===")
print(f"Time: {datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat()}Z")
print()

checks = [
    ("All production dashboards are showing baseline data (no missing panels)", True),
    ("Canary deployment completed successfully (0 errors in canary pods)", True),
    ("All on-call engineers are reachable (SRE + ML Eng confirmed via Slack)", True),
    ("No active production incidents in the cluster", True),
    ("LLM API vendor status page shows all-green", True),
    ("Rollback procedure confirmed: feature flag tested within last hour", True),
]

all_green = True
results = []
for question, required in checks:
    answer = ask(question)
    results.append({"check": question, "answer": answer, "required": required})
    if required and not answer:
        all_green = False
        print(f"  => NO-GO: This check must be YES to proceed.")

decision = "GO" if all_green else "NO-GO"
print(f"\n=== DECISION: {decision} ===")

status = json.loads(pathlib.Path("launch-status.json").read_text())
status["go_no_go"] = {
    "decision": decision,
    "timestamp": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
    "checks": results,
    "decided_by": input("Your @handle: ").strip(),
}
pathlib.Path("launch-status.json").write_text(json.dumps(status, indent=2))

if not all_green:
    print("Launch is on HOLD. Resolve failing checks and re-run go_no_go.py.")
    sys.exit(1)

print("Launch is GO. Proceed with traffic shift.")

The go/no-go decision at T-0 should be made by the PRR coordinator, not autonomously by a CI job. Automation ensures the pre-conditions are met; a human makes the final call to send traffic to users. Record the decision and the deciding engineer in launch-status.json for the audit trail.
Summary
Every launch readiness task needs three things to be real: a named @handle owner, a T-minus deadline, and a verifiable definition of done with an evidence artifact.

The 14-day window has four phases with dependencies: T-14 (load test, full eval), T-7 (runbook drill, security review, cost projection), T-3 (model pin, on-call, waivers), T-1 (dashboard, all sign-offs, final gate).
Wire CI domain jobs to call update_launch_status.py to machine-write launch-status.json — the final gate job blocks the production deploy if all_hard_blockers_green is false.
Run check_launch_assignments.py at T-12 to catch unassigned tasks; every task must have a @handle by the T-14 kickoff meeting.
The final gate workflow requires a manual LAUNCH confirmation input and reads launch-status.json to verify all hard-blocker tasks are PASS before the production deploy proceeds.

The PRR coordinator owns the process but approves no task; their responsibility is ensuring every task has an owner, every deadline is tracked, and any blocker is escalated with enough runway to fix it.
